text:
This paper presents NetworkRepository (NR), the first web-based data repository for supporting real-time interactive graph analytics and visualization. To the best of our knowledge, current data repositories such as SNAP and the UCI Machine Learning Repository function primarily as static sources of downloadable data. In contrast, NetworkRepository enables users to interactively explore, visualize, and compare networks in their web browsers. Users can analyze data in various facets of the network in real time, revealing patterns that are often not clear from static visualizations.

Several feature rich visualization and analysis tools have been developed to facilitate the use of NetworkRepository. However, it is the large number of datasets within the repository that truly differentiates NR. Rather than being a handful of datasets within a simple interface, NetworkRepository currently contains hundreds of datasets from many different fields. In an effort to foster scientific collaboration within the network science community, we have implemented social networking features within the interface. These features allow users to discuss specific datasets, share their own visualizations of the data, leave comments and observations, and even collaborate on new analyses. Ultimately, NetworkRepository aims to bridge the significant gap between data storage and actual data analysis, and we believe it provides a unique and valuable platform for network science research.
